title: Association between prenatal glucocorticoid exposure and adolescent neurodevelopment: An observational follow-up study.
ehr_evidence: The study is a prospective observational follow-up using direct clinical measurements (IQ tests, EEG, stress paradigms) rather than EHR or linked health data.
summary: A prospective observational follow-up study investigating the long-term effects of prenatal betamethasone exposure on neurodevelopmental, metabolic, and autonomic outcomes in adolescents.
key_findings: No statistically significant group difference in global IQ, ADHD symptoms, metabolic markers, HPAA activity, or ANS activity.
Reduced electrocortical activity in the frontal brain region in the betamethasone group.
population: 21 adolescents (14-15 years old) exposed to prenatal betamethasone; 35 reference children
data_availability_statement: Additional supporting information can be found online in the Supporting Information section at the end of this article.
